chunks.text: November 1, 2017: Amit Kumar and Accel launch Accel Scholars EECS mentorship program. EECS alumnus Amit Kumar (B.S. ’03) and the venture firm Accel are launching a mentorship program called Accel Scholars to support EECS undergraduates. Accel will work with a select group of students over the course of a year, hosting networking dinners and also guaranteeing the students an internship at a portfolio company.
chunks.question: What mentorship program did Amit Kumar and Accel launch?

chunks.text: October 26, 2017: Mattel releases Dash foldable robot bugs. Mattel has launched a line of biologically inspired foldable robot bugs designed in collaboration with Dash Robots, a spin-off of the Biomimetic Millisystems Lab (BML). The researchers at BML, under the direction of Prof. Ron Fearing, draw inspiration from nature to build more efficient robotics. The new toys are called Dash foldable robot bugs.
chunks.question: What did Mattel release in collaboration with Dash Robots?

chunks.text: October 26, 2017: Rebecca Chery meets PREP design challenge. The experiences of EECS freshman Rebecca Chery, a participant in the Pre-Engineering Program (PREP), are described in a Berkeley Engineering article titled “PREP by design.” PREP is a three-week program that gives incoming engineering majors a head start on academics, networking and professional development. Chery’s team used equipment at the university for their project.
chunks.question: What program did Rebecca Chery participate in?

chunks.text: October 2, 2017: Jimmy Liu and Zuhayeer Musa build the future. CS majors Jimmy Liu and Zuhayeer Musa are featured in a Berkeley News article titled “In undergrad startup class, students learn to build the future.” Liu and Musa co-founded a startup called Bash while still in high school. When they came to Cal, they partnered with CS Prof. Scott Shenker to further develop their venture.
chunks.question: What startup did Jimmy Liu and Zuhayeer Musa co-found?

chunks.text: September 28, 2017: Shahin Farshchi on making the ‘impossible’ possible through feats of engineering. EECS alumnus Shahin Farshchi (B.S. ’02) is the subject of an Iranian Americans’ Contributions Project (IACP) interview. Farshchi is currently a Partner at Lux Capital Management where he has invested in various technology companies. The interview explores his personal and professional background as a prominent Iranian-American contributor.
chunks.question: Where does Shahin Farshchi currently work?
chunks.answer: Lux Capital Management as a Partner.

chunks.text: September 20, 2017: Arvind Sridhar awarded Davidson Fellows scholarship. Management, Entrepreneurship, & Technology Program (M.E.T.) student Arvind Sridhar (CS/Business) has been awarded a $25,000 Davidson Fellows scholarship. The award is presented annually by the Davidson Institute for Talent Development to 20 students based on significant work in fields like science and technology. Sridhar’s scholarship recognizes his achievements in his dual-degree program.
chunks.question: What scholarship did Arvind Sridhar receive?

chunks.text: September 11, 2017: Scott Shenker wins 2017 Berkeley Visionary Award. CS Prof. Scott Shenker has won a 2017 Visionary Award from the Berkeley Chamber of Commerce. The award acknowledges entrepreneurs and celebrates people with imagination and persistence to innovate in Berkeley. Shenker co-founded Nicira, a company focused on software-defined networking (SDN) and network virtualization technologies.
chunks.question: What award did Scott Shenker win in 2017?

chunks.text: September 8, 2017: Berkeley ranks second in most venture capital-backed entrepreneurs in 2017. For the second year in a row, U.C. Berkeley has ranked No. 2 among the 50 undergraduate programs that produce the most venture capital-backed entrepreneurs, according to PitchBook’s 2017-18 report. The report compares undergraduate and MBA programs, analyzing data such as companies founded and capital raised by alumni.
chunks.question: What ranking did U.C. Berkeley achieve in 2017?

chunks.text: August 30, 2017: Berkeley team takes 3rd place in Greylock Hackfest. Undergraduate students Jian Lu (EECS junior), Walt Leung (CS sophomore), Jiayi Chen (CS junior), and Malhar Patel (EECS junior) placed 3rd at the Greylock Hackfest in July. Their platform, BeAR, allows multiple users to connect to the same augmented reality (AR) session. The Hackfest was sponsored by Greylock Partners, a venture capital firm.
chunks.question: What platform did the Berkeley team develop for the Hackfest?

chunks.text: August 25, 2017: M.E.T. program welcomes inaugural class. The Management, Engineering, & Technology (M.E.T.) program welcomed its inaugural class of 40 students this week, drawn from about 2,500 applicants. Undergrads admitted to M.E.T. combine courses at the Haas School of Business with one of three engineering tracks, including EECS. They take classes in both subjects throughout their undergraduate studies to prepare for leadership roles in technology and business.
chunks.question: How many students were in the inaugural M.E.T. class?
